name: Lemon Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
LEMON FROSTING
Zest of 1 medium sized lemon
1/3 Cup Granulated sugar
2 Packages cream cheese -- room temperature
2 Tablespoons Fresh lemon juice
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Lightly grease two 8-inch layer cake pans.
Line the bottoms with waxed paper and lightly grease the paper. Dust the pans with a little flour and shake out any excess.
2. In a small bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. In another small bowl, mix the lemon juice and vanilla extract.
4. Finely grind the zest of 1 medium sized lemon in a food processor or blender. Transfer to a large bowl, add softened butter, and beat with an electric mixer until pale and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, ensuring each is incorporated before adding the next.
5. Alternately add the flour mixture and lemon juice (beginning and ending with flour), mixing on low speed after each addition. Pour batter into prepared pans.
6. Bake for 40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean. Allow to cool on a wire rack for 20 minutes, then invert cakes and remove waxed paper.
7. To make the frosting: Finely grind the zest of 1 medium sized lemon in a food processor or blender. In a medium bowl, beat the cream cheese mixture until smooth (this can also be done in the food processor). Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for about 1 hour, or until firm but spreadable.
8. To assemble: Using a long, sharp knife, slice each cake horizontally into four thin layers. Frost between layers using about 1/3 cup frosting, then frost the outside. Refrigerate before serving.
